title: Humanizing the unmet need in rare disease_Case Study
content:
Case Studies 01 / 05 Next case
Pharvaris Case Study — Body
Case Study 05 · Pharvaris

Activating patients to drive engagement and persistence in rare disease

Client
Pharvaris
BPD's Role
AOR
Audience
HCPs, Investor Community, Patients/Care Partners
Business Objective

Identify, engage, and motivate a highly fragmented and difficult-to-reach rare disease patient population by driving meaningful engagement with disease and treatment education to build a base of qualified, high-intent patients and caregivers, successfully positioning the brand as the next evolution in care, ahead of product launch

Situation

The brand was preparing to launch in a very rare disease category where patients were difficult to identify and segment. Treatment options had expanded over the past decade, but: still required trade-offs (efficacy vs. tolerability, convenience vs control) leaving patients and HCPs settling for “good enough” outcomes.

Approach

BPD designed a patient-first engagement strategy built on emotional resonance, belief change, and pre-launch activation. Key components included:

  • 01 Reframed the Category Around “Unmet Need Still Exists”

    Highlighted the gaps in current treatments while validating patient frustrations and compromises, creating permission to expect better outcomes

  • 02 Developed Deeply Personal, Relatable Storytelling

    Built authentic patient narratives reflecting real-life challenges

  • 03 Built a Targeted Digital Engagement Ecosystem

    To overcome identification challenges, we deployed precision-targeted digital campaigns, created high-value educational content

  • 04 Activated Patients and HCPs in Parallel

    Recognizing dual influence on treatment decisions, we delivered patient-facing messaging that encouraged self-advocacy conversations with providers while supporting HCP awareness

  • 05 Built a Pre-Launch “Ready-to-Act” Audience

    Rather than waiting for approval, we created a base of informed, engaged patients, positioning the brand as a trusted source of information with a future solution worth anticipating

The Impact +0 Qualified opt-ins in the first 6 months +0 Website engagement 0 To annual opt-in goal by Q3
Creative

title: Positioning beyond efficacy_Case Study
content:
Case Studies 01 / 05 Next case
Elevar Therapeutics Case Study — Body
Case Study 04 · Elevar Therapeutics

Differentiating a combination therapy in a crowded oncology market through integrated strategy and accelerated launch execution

Client
Elevar Therapeutics
BPD's Role
AOR
Audience
HCPs, Investor Community, Patients/Care Partners
Business Objective

Prepare for the launch of a combination oncology therapy by establishing clear differentiation in a highly competitive market. The objective included overcoming perceived limitations in efficacy data driven by study design, aligning cross-functional teams to deliver a unified, credible value story, and accelerating adoption across both academic and community oncology settings

Situation

The brand was preparing to launch a combination therapy into a crowded oncology landscape where clinical differentiation was nuanced and highly scrutinized. Efficacy data, while compelling, faced skepticism due to study design considerations, creating key challenges including perception barriers and fragmented treatment paradigms across academic and community setting. Accelerated Timelines demanded purposeful alignment across medical affairs, commercial/brand and market access teams to prepare for launch under compressed timelines while ensuring strategic alignment

Approach

BPD led a fully integrated launch strategy that aligned scientific credibility, cross-functional execution, and real-world adoption dynamics.

  • 01 Reframed the Clinical Narrative to Address Perception Gaps

    Translated complex efficacy data into clinically meaningful outcomes, directly addressing concerns related to study design

  • 02 Leveraged KOL Influence to Build Confidence and Credibility

    Activated established KOL relationships to validate clinical interpretation and reinforce confidence in treatment approach

  • 03 Bridged Academic and Community Oncology Adoption Gaps

    Recognizing differences in treatment behavior: and creating targeted peer-to-peer and personal engagements

  • 04 Orchestrated Cross-Functional Teams Under Accelerated Timelines

    BPD acted as the integration layer across internal teams, ensuring Medical, Commercial, and Access teams were strategically aligned and operating from a single narrative framework. Internal insights were captured and translated into strategy, with continuous optimization

The Impact +0 MoM site visits +0 Engagement with site content YoY 0 CTR above benchmark achieved in one month

title: Community Oncology Activation_Case Study
content:
Case Studies 01 / 05 Next case
Catalyst Pharmaceuticals Case Study — Body
Case Study 03 · Catalyst Pharmaceuticals

Activating community oncology to accelerate diagnosis and treatment

Client
Catalyst Pharmaceuticals
BPD's Role
AOR
Audience
HCPs, Investor Community, Patients/Care Partners
Business Objective

Increase recognition and diagnosis of Cancer-Associated Lambert-Eaton Myasthenic Syndrome (CA-LEMS) in the oncology setting by improving diagnostic accuracy, expanding appropriate testing and driving early intervention

Situation

The clinical reality is that CA-LEMS is a rare paraneoplastic syndrome linked to SCLC that is often misdiagnosed and poorly understood in oncology settings

Barriers:

  • Low awareness among community oncologists
  • Misinterpretation of symptoms
  • Limited diagnostic testing adoption
  • Fragmented referral pathways

Resulting in patients being diagnosed late and treated suboptimally

Approach

BPD focused on activating the real-world point of care community setting through our established HCP network, while leveraging the KOL voices to support an evolved treatment paradigm. Key elements included:

  • 01 Disease Architecture Mapping

    Connected neurology, oncology, and diagnostic pathways

  • 02 Clinical Signal Amplification

    Identified subtle indicators to improve recognition

  • 03 Community Oncology Activation

    Targeted education and engagement where diagnosis and treatment decisions occur

  • 04 Access & Pathway Integration

    Engaged decision-makers to reduce reimbursement barriers and support pathway inclusion

Strategic Insight

CA-LEMS isn’t just missed because it’s rare. It’s missed because:

  • It doesn’t fit oncologists’ primary focus
  • Its symptoms reinforce expected cancer narratives
  • Time constraints discourage second-guessing

Oncologists are trained to focus on cancer. When symptoms like weakness and fatigue appear, they fit the expected disease narrative, so they are accepted, not questioned.

The challenge wasn’t awareness alone — it was interpretation.

Creative Approach

Drive testing through disease awareness Treat with FIRDAPSE

Drive testing through disease awareness

Leave No Stone Unturned

The campaign translated this clinical challenge into a powerful visual metaphor: the physical weight of symptoms patients carry when CA-LEMS goes unrecognized. The call-to-action urged oncologists to leave no stone unturned when patients with SCLC present with unexplained symptoms.

At the center of the campaign, patients were depicted burdened by literal stones—visually representing the progressive weakness and fatigue that make everyday movements like standing or climbing stairs increasingly difficult. These striking visuals made the invisible visible, bringing urgency and humanity to a condition often misinterpreted as cancer. The creative headline is a rallying cry to change behaviors: Leave no stone unturned

Treat with FIRDAPSE — Launch

Evolution of Dan

The Evolution of Dan campaign highlights the progression from immobility to movement when Cancer-Associated LEMS with SCLC is treated with FIRDAPSE. The hero is depicted in a sequence showing his transformation from sitting solemnly and unable to move, to gradually rising and participating in important moments in life. These striking visuals bring to life hope and promise to a condition that robs patients of mobility. The headline “The Evolution of Dan” connects directly to the brand promise that FIRDAPSE can help patients stay on their feet, representing not just physical improvement but the meaningful step forward that allows patients to be present for what matters most.

Branded web experiences

Measures of Success

title: Oncology Product Launch_Case Study
content:
Case Studies 01 / 05 Next case
Accord BioPharma Case Study — Body
Case Study 02 · Accord BioPharma

Driving targeted adoption of a GnRH agonist in a commoditized market

Client
Accord BioPharma
BPD's Role
AOR
Audience
HCPs, Investor Community, Patients/Care Partners
Business Objective

Successfully introduce a GnRH agonist into a highly-commoditized, established category by identifying and activating high-opportunity segments for switching

Situation

The brand entered a GnRH agonist market characterized by strong incumbent therapies with high physician familiarity and trust. Clinicians perceived the available options as clinically equivalent, with prescribing driven largely by habit, workflow, familiarity, and operational ease. Key realities included that physicians were not actively seeking alternatives. Switching required justification beyond efficacy and minimal disruption to practice, making competing directly for full market leadership an unrealistic near-term objective.

Approach

BPD developed a focused, precision-based launch strategy centered on removing friction and amplifying real-world value without overextending positioning beyond what the market would accept

  • 01 Reframed Value Around Delivery & Practice Experience

    Rather than competing on molecule or efficacy, we elevated the importance of administration experience, workflow efficiency, and practice burden

  • 02 Identified and Activated “Low-Hanging Fruit” Segments

    Instead of broad targeting, we focused on providers experiencing, scheduling friction, high patient volume, operational inefficiencies, and practices open to incremental improvement and workflow optimization

  • 03 Built a Clear, Credible Switching Rationale

    We aligned messaging around clinical confidence, reinforcing reliability and operational benefit

  • 04 Maintained Strategic Discipline in Positioning

    We focused on fit-for-purpose value, reinforced where the product delivered meaningful advantage

The Impact +0 impressions +0 Exceeded sales target in yr 1
Creative

title: Launching in breast cancer Case Study
content:
Case Studies 01 / 05 Next case
Athenex Case Study — Body
Case Study 01 · Athenex Oncology

Reframing the breast cancer treatment landscape to drive adoption

Client
Athenex Oncology
BPD's Role
AOR
Audience
HCPs, Investor Community, Patients/Care Partners
Business Objective

Establish category leadership and accelerate adoption by creating a unified, clinically grounded brand narrative that improves clarity of line of therapy, patient identification, and prescribing confidence

Situation

In breast oncology, communication is often fragmented across corporate positioning, disease education, and individual product messaging. This fragmentation leads to confusion in treatment sequencing, inconsistent understanding of where therapies fit, and slower adoption due to a lack of clarity. In an established category, lack of clarity over efficacy limits growth

Approach

BPD built a fully-integrated oncology narrative system to align how the market understands the category and the product’s role within it. Key components included:

  • 01 Scientific Narrative Development

    Defined the clinical problem and clarified treatment decision drivers

  • 02 Unified Brand Architecture

    Connected corporate credibility, disease leadership, and product positioning into one cohesive story

  • 03 Cross-Functional Messaging Alignment

    Ensured consistency across medical affairs, commercial, corporate and patient engagement

  • 04 HCP Education and Engagement Strategy

    Delivered targeted education to improve understanding of treatment placement and patient eligibility

The Impact Intent to prescribe +0 Increase among target HCPs Accurate understanding of line-of-therapy placement +0 in post-marketing surveys Eligible patient identification +0 in community practices HCP engagement with clinical education +0 in the 1st year

*KPIs are averaged based on proprietary market research results across each campaign.

04Establishing a Presence

Establishing a presence in the breast cancer space.

Branded the division to reinforce the focus and dedication to the oncology space. Developed 3 distinct campaigns to strategically establish Athenex Oncology (AO) as part of the metastatic breast cancer (mBC) community, positioning their first product for success.

01 Empowered to be Bold Corporate Awareness 02 Facing mBC Together Unmet Need · Community Activation 03 CIPN Never-y Day Disease Awareness 04 Launch of Product Launch
Campaign 01

Empowered to be Bold.

Corporate Awareness

Positioned AO as an innovator in oncology by highlighting their mission, values, and approach to developing therapies.

We're mastering the art of patient-focused treatment. Our robust investigational pipeline of novel small molecule, biologic, and cellular therapies is focused on both improving outcomes and improving the cancer treatment experience for patients around the world.

Campaign 02

Facing mBC Together.

Unmet Need · Community Activation

Established AO's relationship with the mBC community through a campaign that addressed the mental and emotional isolation of the disease.

Campaign 03

CIPN Never-y Day.

Disease Awareness

Brought awareness to chemo-induced peripheral neuropathy, priming the market for the new product.

Campaign 04 · Launch

Launch of product.

After three campaigns priming the corporate, community, and disease layers, AO's first product launched inside a story the audience was already part of.

content:

The human spirit
remains undefeated.

Client
AdventHealth
Market

Never Underestimate the Human Spirit

Real stories of resilience, showing what happens when body, mind and spirit are treated as one.

Our Campaign

01 Problem

AdventHealth has been the pioneer of whole-person care for more than a century. But with messages of holistic body, mind and spirit care flooding the marketplace, our leadership and voice were getting lost in the sauce. We needed to remind the world that everyone deserves to feel whole.

02 Idea

We positioned AdventHealth as the official champion of the body, mind and human spirit by showing medical marvels that happen every single day. In a world of artificial hype, we relentlessly featured real patients, real providers and real stories of triumph, victory and hope — all while putting real belief back at the center of care.

03 Solution

AdventHealth reclaimed ownership of whole-person care and built equity in our "feel whole" promise. Brand awareness has grown year over year in every market.

title: AdventHealth Be the Light Case Study
content:
Case Studies 01 / 07 Next
Case Study · AdventHealth

Live your purpose.
Shine bright.

Client
AdventHealth
Market
National
https://bpdhealthcare.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/AdventHealth-Employer-Brand-Recruitment-Be-The-Light-ENG-30_YT-copy.mp4

Be the Light

AdventHealth tapped into why healthcare workers choose this work in the first place. A platform that elevates every role, giving purpose a presence and making the invisible impact of care impossible to ignore.

Our Campaign

01 Problem

Like many health systems in 2025, AdventHealth needed to hire more nurses, clinicians and healthcare professionals. Shortages were still dragging post-COVID, and the usual recruitment playbook wasn't enough.

02 Idea

AdventHealth's brand platform, "Feel Whole," was gaining steam across markets, and the employer brand had to keep up. We extended the employer brand to under-recognized healthcare roles and crafted a bold employer brand platform called "Be the Light," rooted in the truth that people in this field aren't just looking for work. They're answering something deeper.

03 Solution

We crafted a spoken-word film that led with texture and humanity. Light was a visual thematic thread throughout, reflecting AdventHealth's faith-based mission. We expanded the cast beyond the typical doctors and nurses often seen in healthcare creative and put technicians, specialists and a myriad of behind-the-scenes roles front and center. The creative enabled the idea to go far beyond an intrinsic feeling and show the outward impact — the light — that healthcare professionals have at AdventHealth every single day.

Channels
  • Film

Partnership Results

title: Community Health Innovations Case Study
content:
Case Studies 01 / 07 Next
Case Study · Community Health Innovations

Diabetes can be a
beast of a disease.

Client
Community Health Innovations
Market
Monterey County, CA
https://vimeo.com/1046941611?fl=pl&fe=vl

Don't Feed the Diabetes

A personification of diabetes, the threat at hand.

Our Campaign

01 Problem

Known for its spectacular coastline, Monterey County has another claim to fame: the highest diabetes rate in California. With nearly half of residents having type-2 diabetes or pre-diabetes, Community Health Innovations (CHI), the county's population health organization, knew it needed to act. With our findings, they realized the real barrier to better health in Monterey County wasn't awareness. It was misinformation.

02 Idea

We created "The Beast" character — a bold idea that reframes the risk of diabetes and pushes people to see the threat for what it really is.

03 Solution

Backed by a robust media strategy, culturally aware marketing and an educational website with tools like a health risk assessment and hospital resources, the "Don't Feed the Diabetes" campaign reframed the disease as a serious but preventable threat. "The Beast" became a memorable representation of diabetes that made a lasting impact.

title: SHIFT Case Study
content:

Case Study · Robert Wood Johnson Foundation

Giving nurses the
voice they deserve.

Client
Robert Wood Johnson Foundation
Market
National
https://vimeo.com/1187010055/70dd5fbc99?share=copy&fl=sv&fe=ci

SHIFT

SHIFT is a platform built for nurses to speak freely, connect honestly and turn shared experience into momentum for real change.

Our Campaign

01 Problem

Health disparities. Inequities. The healthcare industry needs to change. Yet no one has figured out a solution. Maybe it's time nurses had a voice.

02 Idea

The Robert Wood Johnson Foundation (RWJF) engaged nurses on a personal level with deep audience data and research. We created SHIFT, an online community for frontline nurses that dug deep into the dynamics shaping race in the nursing industry and in healthcare overall.

03 Solution

The campaign launched in 2020 with a groundbreaking podcast, web content and social content, creating a rich online community for nurses — a place for nurses to gather, share, laugh, think and cry without fear or judgment.

The community platform expanded in 2022 with the documentary film Who Cares, featuring Native American nurse Whitney Fear's fight to heal lives in the Oglala Dakota Nation of South Dakota, home to the lowest life expectancy in the United States.

title: UNC Health Case Study
content:
Case Studies 01 / 07 Next
Case Study · UNC Health

We live for everyday moments.

Client
UNC Health
Market
Raleigh-Durham Area, NC
https://bpdhealthcare.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/24-UNCHC-153-UNC_Everyday-Moments_v10.mp4

Everyday Greatness

UNC Health turned academic medicine into something you can feel. Real moments. Real people. Real world-class care that shows up quietly and consistently. The kind of greatness you don't have to be told to believe.

Our Campaign

01 Problem

UNC Health had recently unified its brand, transitioning from UNC Health Care to UNC Health in 2020. It needed to build awareness and preference for this new identity while competing against well-established players like Duke Health and WakeMed. At the same time, the system faced added pressure to drive oncology growth, support talent recruitment and ensure its cancer story strengthened the broader brand.

02 Idea

"Everyday Greatness" is the belief that extraordinary care isn't reserved for rare moments. It shows up every day, in ways both big and small. It reframes UNC Health's academic excellence as human and accessible, translating world-class expertise into meaningful, everyday impact. Oncology becomes a powerful expression of this promise, where breakthrough science and compassionate care meet in the moments that matter most.

03 Solution

"Everyday Greatness" became a unifying platform connecting UNC Health's brand and oncology ambitions under a single, emotionally resonant idea. The campaign made academic excellence feel human and relatable, showing how extraordinary care appears in everyday moments. By positioning oncology as an extension of the brand, it strengthened overall preference while supporting service line growth — giving patients and prospective employees a clearer reason to choose UNC Health.

Channels
  • Film
  • Print
  • Radio
  • Digital

Better health takes
more than healthcare.

Client
UC Davis Health
Market
San Francisco Bay Area, CA
https://bpdhealthcare.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/UCDH0121000H_UCDavisHealth_NewLifeAndHope_30.mp4

Believe in Better

UC Davis Health made complex care feel closer. A more human idea that connects breakthroughs to everyday health. Building trust, driving growth and proving better care should actually feel better.

Our Campaign

01 Problem

UC Davis Health faced perception challenges that were limiting growth. Many consumers were unclear about primary care access, insurance coverage and how its complex care compared to other Bay Area providers. Research revealed a deep truth: people want to believe health inequities can be addressed, but they need to see meaningful action to believe change is possible.

02 Idea

Using BPD's INCITE strategy framework, we repositioned UC Davis Health around a more human, purpose-driven brand idea centered on inspiring better health for everyone, not just its patients.

03 Solution

The "Believe in Better" campaign connects UC Davis Health's clinical excellence with everyday health in a more relatable, human way. The campaign strengthened UC Davis Health's reputation for complex care while also supporting growth in primary and routine services. By focusing on health equity and the social factors that shape well-being, it repositioned UC Davis Health as a system taking meaningful action to remove barriers and make better health possible for all.

The Better
Way to Better

Baptist Health transformed same-day care by creating a path to a better experience.

Our Campaign

01 Problem

Same-day care is rarely a decision. It is proximity, urgency and habit. You go wherever is closest, expecting the same long wait, the same cold experience. At some point, people stop comparing. They just endure it.

02 Idea

We turned the red carpet, long associated with first-class treatment, green. This marked a clear, visible path to care that feels considered, intentional and elevated.

03 Solution

We rolled out the green carpet across many touchpoints, making it impossible to miss in the community. Our messaging was also tailored to the Hispanic audience with "El mejor camino para tu bienestar." That's how Baptist Health turned an experience people dreaded into one they didn't expect.

title: Market Research
content:
Healthcare Consumers are Changing. We’re Tracking It in Real Time.

A recurring pulse on the healthcare consumer designed to help forward-thinking health systems grow what’s next. Healthcare is at an inflection point. Margin pressure is intensifying. Consumer expectations are rising. Competitive forces are accelerating. Yet most health systems are still operating on lagging indicators. The BPD Research Initiative is new consumer research program that provides a recurring, first-party view into how healthcare consumers are thinking, choosing, and acting before those behaviors show up in market share.

What is it?

Throughout the year, we field proprietary surveys of healthcare consumers across the country, uncovering insights into:
  • Care-seeking behavior
  • Service line decision drivers
  • Price sensitivity and financial confidence
  • Digital and AI expectations
  • Brand perception and loyalty dynamics
From this data, we’re sharing actionable intelligence with our clients and the industry to connect consumer behavior directly to enterprise growth strategy. EXPLORE THE FINDINGS

The Patient Next Door

Meet the new healthcare consumer

This report draws on findings from the Q4 2025 wave to explore the forces reshaping healthcare decisions, from financial pressure and convenience-driven access to the growing influence of digital tools and healthcare communications. What emerges is a clear shift: consumers are taking a more active role in deciding when, where, and whether to seek care, often before engaging with providers — with significant impact to the financial sustainability of health systems. title: Market Assessment Tool
content:
MARKET ASSESSMENT TOOL

See your market clearly. Grow strategically.

BPD’s market assessment tool gives healthcare organizations a precise view of where demand exists, how it’s shifting, and where to focus for growth.

MAT combines consumer insights, campaign performance, and engagement data to create a unified picture of your market. Powered by 1,200+ data points across demographic, behavioral, and social factors, it reveals not just what’s happening—but where opportunity lives.

What you can do

title: ChristianaCare | BPD
content:
Thank You

Thank you for the continued opportunity to explore a partnership with ChristianaCare; our team is more energized than ever by our shared vision of ChristianaCare’s ability to transform how your neighbors experience healthcare through a lens of love and excellence.

OUR APPROACH

We’ve taken your feedback to heart: while our strategic foundation remains the same, we recognize the creative must be both deeply rooted in your Delaware heritage and versatile enough to resonate across the diverse landscape of Delaware County. At BPD, we believe for brands to maximize their potential, they need to be relevant to the greatest number of people, emotionally moving those people and the needle for your brand. But in order to do this, they first must grab your attention.

According to research from WARC, the unfortunate reality is that 85% of ads fail to reach the “attention threshold" that is needed to make an enduring impact*.

The new concepts you’ll find below are designed to shatter the “sea of sameness” in healthcare branding — creating a distinct, unmistakable identity that ensures when your audience sees it they’ll know it’s ChristianaCare before they even see the logo.

As we discussed in our time together, we believe the strongest creative is never something simply selected off the shelf. It’s rooted in collaboration and shaped through perspective, conversation, and partnership. Our role is to bring strategic clarity, fresh thinking, and creative ambition to the table, and then co-create with you to shape an expression that feels unmistakably ChristianaCare and true to the communities you serve. As your neighbor, we’re excited by this opportunity to build something meaningful together.

*WARC, 'The Anatomy of Effectiveness,' presented at Cannes Lions 2022.

BUILDING ON WHAT WE HEARD

What We’re Carrying Forward

A strategy grounded in ChristianaCare’s brand and ambition. Our understanding of what matters most to your organization and neighbors you serve came through clearly.

Care and love are our defining emotional truths. It reinforces the humanity behind our clinical excellence.

“You’ll Know” resonated for feeling distinct and ownable to ChristianaCare. Our strongest idea had a tone and confidence that only you can own.

Where We’re Stretching Further

The nuances of DelCo need to come through authentically. Our new concept must reflect the socioeconomic and cultural diversity of the community.

Tone and imagery should reflect the care behind the brand. The new concept should communicate care and excellence while being mindful of the use of clinical imagery.

The new concept must feel unmistakably ChristianaCare. It should express the brand in a way that is clear, ownable and uniquely yours.

Our strategy is informed by consumer insights across ChristianaCare’s current and expansion markets, and grounded in decades of experience.

Our positioning reflects the earnest needs of your neighbors, while recognizing the growing competitive pressures from local community systems like MainLine Health and leading academic medical centers like Penn Medicine.

In this environment, ChristianaCare has an opportunity to build on its longstanding mission and values, by evolving love from a sentiment into a force that is human, relentless, and visible in action. The recommended strategy dictates that love in healthcare cannot simply be stated; it must be demonstrated in ways the community sees and feels in every interaction.

Creative Directions

Please hover and click on the selections below to view the creative work.  

In the following, you will see creative directions that reflect and highlight your brand with relevant messaging, brand typeface, iconography, and aesthetics with your color palette. The ideas are for proof of concept only, as the photography is not final. Ultimately, the creative will be a collaboration with you that is a co-owned and co-created expression of who ChristianaCare is.

1 2

4 REASONS WHY BPD IS THE RIGHT PARTNER FOR CHRISTIANACARE

INTEGRATED PARTNERSHIP & UNIFIED STRATEGY

Unlike agencies that silo services, BPD offers fully integrated solutions across multiple disciplines, solving business challenges end-to-end. Our work isn’t just about visibility or creative awards — it’s structured to move business metrics, providing outcomes that tie directly to growth and strategic success.

BPD will provide ChristianaCare with a singular fully integrated team where everyone is working on a singular integrated strategy to ensure continuity across campaigns and efficiency in execution, eliminating handoffs between partners from strategy to creative to media and communications.

ADVANCE CONSUMER INSIGHTS & SEGMENTATION

BPD doesn’t start from scratch; we “start on third base.” No one has more data than us. Full stop. Our Proprietary Data Platform, built by our data scientists, gives us over 240 million patient files and 800+ data points per file all buoyed by our AI innovation, highly targeted Precision product, and dynamic delivery.

ChristianaCare needs a sharpened segmentation strategy for your geographic expansion. BPD has already pulled market data showing 62% of Delaware County (Delco) residents and 64% of Delaware State residents lack a PCP.

ELEVATING REPUTATION & THOUGHT LEADERSHIP

BPD goes beyond standard marketing by offering enterprise reputation management, helping C-suites shape public perception, mitigate risk, and lead national conversations.

In order to elevate executive visibility and ChristianaCare’s reputation for clinical excellence BPD has already built a custom thought leadership platform centered on five specific pillars to differentiate you from competitors who only address these topics in silos.

MARKETING OPERATIONS & ROI ACCOUNTABILITY BPD operates with a measurement-first mindset, aligning marketing directly to C-suite business goals to deliver 5x, 10x, and sometimes 20x marketing ROI. ChristianaCare needs a framework for operational discipline and data-driven decision-making. BPD offers near real-time access to advanced marketing analytics, dynamic creative optimization, and a custom measurement framework designed to track brand preference in Delaware and brand awareness in Delco.

Scratch Pad

We wanted to give you a sense of what it’s like to partner with us, so here’s a peek behind the curtain at our process. When we find a powerful territory, we look for all of its edges and possibilities, in co-creation with you.  

And because 85%* of ads are ignored, we design messages that are impossible to ignore in healthcare’s sea of sameness.  

This is an exploration of the Way to Love platform brought to life in different ways. Click below.

SCRATCH PAD

ChristanaCare has a deeply rooted history of love and excellence within Delaware, and you now stand at an inflection point defined by growth, competition, and innovation.

To truly meet this moment, ChristianaCare deserves a partner that brings more than just aesthetics; as a partner, BPD provides our unique combination of deep healthcare data, bold creativity, and the courage to use both. This is where we thrive, and it’s the foundation of our track record in delivering transformative results.

The moment to introduce and cement ChristianaCare as the leading health system in the region is now. BPD is ready to move from concept to impact — let’s get started!

Erin Steuben
VP, Growth

Jason Brown CEO Jessica Schmidt President Mindy Adams Chief Creative Officer Stephanie Wierwille EVP, Strategy & Innovation Joshua Donaghue Executive Vice President, Tradigital Media Trish Brawner Executive Vice President, Client Services David Anastas SVP, Data & Analytics


UAMS Technical Proposal Packet Reference Materials (UAMS RFP 2026-0024)

Below are supplemental work examples and visual references that further illustrate the capabilities, strategic approach, and outcomes outlined in our Technical Proposal Packet.

UAMS has outlined clear priorities for this partnership: advancing system-level brand positioning, driving measurable patient acquisition and service line growth, strengthening academic recruitment and research visibility, and building a transparent, performance-driven marketing infrastructure capable of supporting continued expansion across the state, including Northwest Arkansas. The case studies and work examples below were selected intentionally because they reflect these priorities. 

Each example demonstrates how we have partnered with academic medical centers and complex health systems to modernize brand perception, drive measurable patient growth, elevate reputation across clinical and research missions, and build sustainable marketing engines designed to scale.

E.4 Relevant Experience and Qualifications; Question 1

Describe your firm’s experience supporting health care organizations, including academic medical centers, health care specialty service lines, and multi-location systems.

Click the images below to view the full case studies.

Elevating an AMC

This example is especially relevant as UAMS modernizes system-wide branding and expands access across primary and specialty care—balancing your role as a high-acuity academic leader with growth in the everyday services your communities rely on.

System-Wide Public Sentiment Campaign This demonstrates our experience supporting large, multi-site systems where brand, employee engagement, and reputation must stay aligned across diverse markets. As your marketing footprint expands, this level of enterprise coordination is critical to maintaining clarity and impact. High-Impact Men's Health Campaign

As UAMS looks to tie marketing investment to measurable increases in patient volume, this example demonstrates our ability to connect behavioral insight to activation and drive quantifiable growth within compressed timelines.

E.4 Relevant Experience and Qualifications; Question 3

Provide examples of work demonstrating brand refresh or large-scale branding initiatives. 

Click the images below to view the full case studies.

Improving Health Esteem

Like UAMS, FMOLHS faced the challenge of strengthening health confidence statewide. This work shows how a purpose-driven platform—grounded in visible community engagement and integrated outreach—can elevate brand perception while driving system-wide growth.

Building a Non-Promotional Brand Similar to UAMS’ opportunity to build stronger connections across a diverse statewide footprint, Northern Light Health demonstrates how a unifying, emotionally resonant brand platform can align internal stakeholders, build community trust, and translate system strategy into measurable engagement. Building a National Nursing Community As UAMS advances its academic leadership and clinical expertise, SHIFT illustrates how an authentic, mission-driven platform can activate clinicians as credible ambassadors, strengthen reputation, and extend impact beyond traditional marketing channels.

Provide examples of reporting dashboards or performance summaries.

Below is a sample dashboard video to give you a sense of the platform’s functionality and customization capabilities. While the example reflects a standard setup, we collaborate closely with each client to design reporting that aligns with your unique goals, audiences, and KPIs, whether you're looking for simple, high-level insights or more advanced, granular metrics.

E.5 Performance Measurement and Analytics; Question 3

Describe how insights are used to optimize campaigns over time.

Click the image below to view the full case study.

Data-Driven Optimization Delivering a 23:1 ROI

As UAMS looks to expand volume in priority service lines within an increasingly competitive landscape, our work with AdventHealth demonstrates how disciplined use of audience insights, real-time optimization, and coordinated traditional and digital activation can drive measurable growth and deliver clear, defensible revenue ROI.

Delivering the future to healthcare's leading brands 

At BPD, everything we do – business consulting, branding, reputation management, marketing and performance measurement – is technology-enabled, AI-infused, and supported by our Proprietary Data Platform. All to ignite a future with better health for more people.

title: Precision - Tower Health
content:
|

You can conservatively expect 7:1 ROI

88K

Potential patients targeted from among total addressible market.

1,760

New cardiology patients acquired over a 12-18 mo. period.

$500K

Total entry-level investment required, including media placement and campaign development fees.

$3.5M

Contribution margin expected from new patients acquired from marketing investment shown.

BPD’s precision marketing approach is a new paradigm for patient acquisition, offering a transformative way to achieve measurable financial ROI and predictable outcomes. While it requires a higher investment than typical volume-driving strategies, it yields higher, provable results.

Our solution prioritizes quality over quantity, using advanced data modeling and AI-driven optimization to engage the right audiences with highly relevant and motivating content. And it all is founded on our proprietary data platform, which contains more than 800 points of data on 240 million adults in the U.S. While our approach demands a more focused investment in the upfront approach, the payoff is clear: strong contribution margins that clearly shows marketing as an investment in growth, resulting in concentrating resources where they matter most, rather than spreading them thin.

The pro forma provided here is based on an analysis of your market and brand, and reflects an entry-level precision marketing option. We also offer more customizable patient-acquisition solutions, as well as offering that leverage our data capabilities to help health systems with enhanced segmentation, patient loyalty programs and more.

ADVENTHEALTH

Best-in-class patient acquisition campaign drives 23:1 ROI

View Full Project

Precision Marketing FAQs

What is the approach for patient acquisition shown here?

BPD’s patient acquisition solution, what we call Precision Marketing, leverages our proprietary data platform to identify a list of individual consumers with a given propensity to segment and engage with targeted paid media. While it demands a more focused investment in the upfront approach, the payoff is clear: strong contribution margins that clearly show marketing as an investment in growth. Further, this approach focuses on reducing risk and delivering high-impact results by concentrating resources where they matter most, rather than spreading them thin. The pro forma shown here is based on an actual estimation of potential cardiology patients we could drive to your system using our entry-level precision solution. This offers a lower investment to prove this strategy, with more customizable solutions available to drive even higher patient acquisition and ROI.

How do you determine the number of potential patients in the market?

Using our proprietary data platform, which contains more than 800 data points on more than 240 million adults in the U.S., we can accurately estimate a potential addressable potential market specific to your service area. In this case, we’ve used cardiology services for our targeting, but we can apply precision marketing to estimate the number of potential patients in more than three dozen clinical propensities.

How do you know how many patients will be acquired?

Starting with a potential addressable market noted above, there are two steps we take to estimate the number of patients we expect to attract. First, we narrow the target audience to only the top 10-20% of the total audience using a ranking based on criteria like proximity, insurance, claims data and more. The goal here is to only target those potential patients who are most likely to leverage your system for care. Second, we apply an acquisition rate based on historical campaign results. For example, for cardiology marketing, we typically achieve somewhere between a 2-3% conversion rate from targeting to utilization. The entry-level solution shown here reflects with a more conservative range, but actual acquisition rates can be greater when leveraging our customized solutions, leading to a higher ROI.

How did you determine which service line to use for this assessment?

BPD has propensity modeling for more than three dozen clinical needs/service offerings. Those most commonly leveraged by health systems include cardiology, oncology, orthopedics and emergency room services. We typically land on a targeted service line in one of two ways: clients have already identified their high priority service lines that need increased volumes; or, we use our market assessment tool to help identify where the most opportunity lies in a client’s market. For this estimate, we used cardiology knowing that is typically a high priority service line for most systems. We are happy to run another potential addressable market upon request.

Where are you obtaining contribution margins used in your calculations?

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

Using our proprietary data platform, which contains more than 800 data points on more than 240 million adults in the U.S., we can accurately estimate a potential addressable potential market specific to your service area. In this case, we’ve used cardiology services for our targeting, but we can apply precision marketing to estimate the number of potential patients in more than three dozen clinical propensities.

How do you know how many patients will be acquired?

Starting with a potential addressable market noted above, there are two steps we take to estimate the number of patients we expect to attract. First, we narrow the target audience to only the top 10-20% of the total audience using a ranking based on criteria like proximity, insurance, claims data and more. The goal here is to only target those potential patients who are most likely to leverage your system for care. Second, we apply an acquisition rate based on historical campaign results. For example, for cardiology marketing, we typically achieve somewhere between a 2-3% conversion rate from targeting to utilization. The entry-level solution shown here reflects with a more conservative range, but actual acquisition rates can be greater when leveraging our customized solutions, leading to a higher ROI.

How did you determine which service line to use for this assessment?

BPD has propensity modeling for more than three dozen clinical needs/service offerings. Those most commonly leveraged by health systems include cardiology, oncology, orthopedics and emergency room services. We typically land on a targeted service line in one of two ways: clients have already identified their high priority service lines that need increased volumes; or, we use our market assessment tool to help identify where the most opportunity lies in a client’s market. For this estimate, we used cardiology knowing that is typically a high priority service line for most systems. We are happy to run another potential addressable market upon request.

Where are you obtaining contribution margins used in your calculations?

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

For example, for cardiology marketing, we typically achieve somewhere between a 2-3% conversion rate from targeting to utilization. The entry-level solution shown here reflects with a more conservative range, but actual acquisition rates can be greater when leveraging our customized solutions, leading to a higher ROI.

How did you determine which service line to use for this assessment?

BPD has propensity modeling for more than three dozen clinical needs/service offerings. Those most commonly leveraged by health systems include cardiology, oncology, orthopedics and emergency room services. We typically land on a targeted service line in one of two ways: clients have already identified their high priority service lines that need increased volumes; or, we use our market assessment tool to help identify where the most opportunity lies in a client’s market. For this estimate, we used cardiology knowing that is typically a high priority service line for most systems. We are happy to run another potential addressable market upon request.

Where are you obtaining contribution margins used in your calculations?

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

For this estimate, we used cardiology knowing that is typically a high priority service line for most systems. We are happy to run another potential addressable market upon request.

Where are you obtaining contribution margins used in your calculations?

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

Best-in-class patient acquisition campaign drives 23:1 ROI

Using our proprietary data platform, which contains more than 800 data points on more than 240 million adults in the U.S., we can accurately estimate a potential addressable potential market specific to your service area. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. 77K

Potential patients targeted from among total addressible market.

1,533

Total entry-level investment required, including media placement and campaign development fees.

$3.1M

Contribution margin expected from new patients acquired from marketing investment shown.

BPD’s precision marketing approach is a new paradigm for patient acquisition, offering a transformative way to achieve measurable financial ROI and predictable outcomes. While it requires a higher investment than typical volume-driving strategies, it yields higher, provable results.

Our solution prioritizes quality over quantity, using advanced data modeling and AI-driven optimization to engage the right audiences with highly relevant and motivating content. And it all is founded on our proprietary data platform, which contains more than 800 points of data on 240 million adults in the U.S. While our approach demands a more focused investment in the upfront approach, the payoff is clear: strong contribution margins that clearly shows marketing as an investment in growth, resulting in concentrating resources where they matter most, rather than spreading them thin.

The pro forma provided here is based on an analysis of your market and brand, and reflects an entry-level precision marketing option. We also offer more customizable patient-acquisition solutions, as well as offering that leverage our data capabilities to help health systems with enhanced segmentation, patient loyalty programs and more.

ADVENTHEALTH

Best-in-class patient acquisition campaign drives 23:1 ROI

View Full Project

Precision Marketing FAQs

What is the approach for patient acquisition shown here?

BPD’s patient acquisition solution, what we call Precision Marketing, leverages our proprietary data platform to identify a list of individual consumers with a given propensity to segment and engage with targeted paid media. While it demands a more focused investment in the upfront approach, the payoff is clear: strong contribution margins that clearly show marketing as an investment in growth. Further, this approach focuses on reducing risk and delivering high-impact results by concentrating resources where they matter most, rather than spreading them thin. The pro forma shown here is based on an actual estimation of potential cardiology patients we could drive to your system using our entry-level precision solution. This offers a lower investment to prove this strategy, with more customizable solutions available to drive even higher patient acquisition and ROI.

How do you determine the number of potential patients in the market?

Using our proprietary data platform, which contains more than 800 data points on more than 240 million adults in the U.S., we can accurately estimate a potential addressable potential market specific to your service area. In this case, we’ve used cardiology services for our targeting, but we can apply precision marketing to estimate the number of potential patients in more than three dozen clinical propensities.

How do you know how many patients will be acquired?

Starting with a potential addressable market noted above, there are two steps we take to estimate the number of patients we expect to attract. First, we narrow the target audience to only the top 10-20% of the total audience using a ranking based on criteria like proximity, insurance, claims data and more. The goal here is to only target those potential patients who are most likely to leverage your system for care. Second, we apply an acquisition rate based on historical campaign results. For example, for cardiology marketing, we typically achieve somewhere between a 2-3% conversion rate from targeting to utilization. The entry-level solution shown here reflects with a more conservative range, but actual acquisition rates can be greater when leveraging our customized solutions, leading to a higher ROI.

How did you determine which service line to use for this assessment?

BPD has propensity modeling for more than three dozen clinical needs/service offerings. Those most commonly leveraged by health systems include cardiology, oncology, orthopedics and emergency room services. We typically land on a targeted service line in one of two ways: clients have already identified their high priority service lines that need increased volumes; or, we use our market assessment tool to help identify where the most opportunity lies in a client’s market. For this estimate, we used cardiology knowing that is typically a high priority service line for most systems. We are happy to run another potential addressable market upon request.

Where are you obtaining contribution margins used in your calculations?

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

$6M

Contribution margin expected from new patients acquired from marketing investment shown.

BPD’s precision marketing approach is a new paradigm for patient acquisition, offering a transformative way to achieve measurable financial ROI and predictable outcomes. While it requires a higher investment than typical volume-driving strategies, it yields higher, provable results.

Our solution prioritizes quality over quantity, using advanced data modeling and AI-driven optimization to engage the right audiences with highly relevant and motivating content. And it all is founded on our proprietary data platform, which contains more than 800 points of data on 240 million adults in the U.S. While our approach demands a more focused investment in the upfront approach, the payoff is clear: strong contribution margins that clearly shows marketing as an investment in growth, resulting in concentrating resources where they matter most, rather than spreading them thin.

The pro forma provided here is based on an analysis of your market and brand, and reflects an entry-level precision marketing option. We also offer more customizable patient-acquisition solutions, as well as offering that leverage our data capabilities to help health systems with enhanced segmentation, patient loyalty programs and more.

ADVENTHEALTH

Best-in-class patient acquisition campaign drives 23:1 ROI

View Full Project

Precision Marketing FAQs

What is the approach for patient acquisition shown here?

BPD’s patient acquisition solution, what we call Precision Marketing, leverages our proprietary data platform to identify a list of individual consumers with a given propensity to segment and engage with targeted paid media. While it demands a more focused investment in the upfront approach, the payoff is clear: strong contribution margins that clearly show marketing as an investment in growth. Further, this approach focuses on reducing risk and delivering high-impact results by concentrating resources where they matter most, rather than spreading them thin. The pro forma shown here is based on an actual estimation of potential cardiology patients we could drive to your system using our entry-level precision solution. This offers a lower investment to prove this strategy, with more customizable solutions available to drive even higher patient acquisition and ROI.

How do you determine the number of potential patients in the market?

Using our proprietary data platform, which contains more than 800 data points on more than 240 million adults in the U.S., we can accurately estimate a potential addressable potential market specific to your service area. In this case, we’ve used cardiology services for our targeting, but we can apply precision marketing to estimate the number of potential patients in more than three dozen clinical propensities.

How do you know how many patients will be acquired?

Starting with a potential addressable market noted above, there are two steps we take to estimate the number of patients we expect to attract. First, we narrow the target audience to only the top 10-20% of the total audience using a ranking based on criteria like proximity, insurance, claims data and more. The goal here is to only target those potential patients who are most likely to leverage your system for care. Second, we apply an acquisition rate based on historical campaign results. For example, for cardiology marketing, we typically achieve somewhere between a 2-3% conversion rate from targeting to utilization. The entry-level solution shown here reflects with a more conservative range, but actual acquisition rates can be greater when leveraging our customized solutions, leading to a higher ROI.

How did you determine which service line to use for this assessment?

BPD has propensity modeling for more than three dozen clinical needs/service offerings. Those most commonly leveraged by health systems include cardiology, oncology, orthopedics and emergency room services. We typically land on a targeted service line in one of two ways: clients have already identified their high priority service lines that need increased volumes; or, we use our market assessment tool to help identify where the most opportunity lies in a client’s market. For this estimate, we used cardiology knowing that is typically a high priority service line for most systems. We are happy to run another potential addressable market upon request.

Where are you obtaining contribution margins used in your calculations?

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.
