Precision Marketing FAQs

What is the approach for patient acquisition shown here?

BPD’s patient acquisition solution, what we call Precision Marketing, leverages our proprietary data platform to identify a list of individual consumers with a given propensity to segment and engage with targeted paid media. While it demands a more focused investment in the upfront approach, the payoff is clear: strong contribution margins that clearly show marketing as an investment in growth. Further, this approach focuses on reducing risk and delivering high-impact results by concentrating resources where they matter most, rather than spreading them thin. The pro forma shown here is based on an actual estimation of potential cardiology patients we could drive to your system using our entry-level precision solution. This offers a lower investment to prove this strategy, with more customizable solutions available to drive even higher patient acquisition and ROI.

How do you determine the number of potential patients in the market?

Using our proprietary data platform, which contains more than 800 data points on more than 240 million adults in the U.S., we can accurately estimate a potential addressable potential market specific to your service area. In this case, we’ve used cardiology services for our targeting, but we can apply precision marketing to estimate the number of potential patients in more than three dozen clinical propensities.

How do you know how many patients will be acquired?

Starting with a potential addressable market noted above, there are two steps we take to estimate the number of patients we expect to attract. First, we narrow the target audience to only the top 10-20% of the total audience using a ranking based on criteria like proximity, insurance, claims data and more. The goal here is to only target those potential patients who are most likely to leverage your system for care. Second, we apply an acquisition rate based on historical campaign results. For example, for cardiology marketing, we typically achieve somewhere between a 2-3% conversion rate from targeting to utilization. The entry-level solution shown here reflects with a more conservative range, but actual acquisition rates can be greater when leveraging our customized solutions, leading to a higher ROI.

How did you determine which service line to use for this assessment?

BPD has propensity modeling for more than three dozen clinical needs/service offerings. Those most commonly leveraged by health systems include cardiology, oncology, orthopedics and emergency room services. We typically land on a targeted service line in one of two ways: clients have already identified their high priority service lines that need increased volumes; or, we use our market assessment tool to help identify where the most opportunity lies in a client’s market. For this estimate, we used cardiology knowing that is typically a high priority service line for most systems. We are happy to run another potential addressable market upon request.

Where are you obtaining contribution margins used in your calculations?

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

In this case, we’ve used cardiology services for our targeting, but we can apply precision marketing to estimate the number of potential patients in more than three dozen clinical propensities.

How do you know how many patients will be acquired?

Starting with a potential addressable market noted above, there are two steps we take to estimate the number of patients we expect to attract. First, we narrow the target audience to only the top 10-20% of the total audience using a ranking based on criteria like proximity, insurance, claims data and more. The goal here is to only target those potential patients who are most likely to leverage your system for care. Second, we apply an acquisition rate based on historical campaign results. For example, for cardiology marketing, we typically achieve somewhere between a 2-3% conversion rate from targeting to utilization. The entry-level solution shown here reflects with a more conservative range, but actual acquisition rates can be greater when leveraging our customized solutions, leading to a higher ROI.

How did you determine which service line to use for this assessment?

BPD has propensity modeling for more than three dozen clinical needs/service offerings. Those most commonly leveraged by health systems include cardiology, oncology, orthopedics and emergency room services. We typically land on a targeted service line in one of two ways: clients have already identified their high priority service lines that need increased volumes; or, we use our market assessment tool to help identify where the most opportunity lies in a client’s market. For this estimate, we used cardiology knowing that is typically a high priority service line for most systems. We are happy to run another potential addressable market upon request.

Where are you obtaining contribution margins used in your calculations?

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

The entry-level solution shown here reflects with a more conservative range, but actual acquisition rates can be greater when leveraging our customized solutions, leading to a higher ROI.

How did you determine which service line to use for this assessment?

BPD has propensity modeling for more than three dozen clinical needs/service offerings. Those most commonly leveraged by health systems include cardiology, oncology, orthopedics and emergency room services. We typically land on a targeted service line in one of two ways: clients have already identified their high priority service lines that need increased volumes; or, we use our market assessment tool to help identify where the most opportunity lies in a client’s market. For this estimate, we used cardiology knowing that is typically a high priority service line for most systems. We are happy to run another potential addressable market upon request.

Where are you obtaining contribution margins used in your calculations?

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.
How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI? Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. title: Texas Children's
content:
Let's ignite greatness together.

|

Thank you again for the opportunity to be considered as your partner for brand and creative.

This page is designed as a resource to help your team get to know us better and learn a bit more about our work and our thinking. While we’ll discuss much of this in our conversation, we wanted to provide a clear, written reference you can return to at any time.

Here, you’ll find:

The BPD Team in Texas – A little bit more about the BPD team you met on-site.

Case Studies – A few case studies that showcase how our big, bold ideas have come to life.

Thought Leadership – A few pieces we believe may resonate with your team’s goals.

Proposal and Presentation – Downloadable links to BPD's proposal and today's presentation for Texas Children's.

A Little More About BPD – A snapshot of how we help our clients ignite greatness.

We’re incredibly energized by the potential to partner with your team and inspired by the important work you’re already doing. We’d be honored to help tell your story in bigger, bolder ways. If anything here or in our conversation sparks questions or ideas, we’d love to explore them with you.


The BPD Team in Texas

At BPD, partnering with us means gaining a deeply engaged, cross-functional team that operates as a true extension of your own. The leaders shown below are the team members you’ve met on-site and represent part of the group who will support your account moving forward. Behind them, we assemble a handpicked Core Team built specifically for your goals, scope, and organizational needs. This team combines account and project management leads with subject matter experts in strategy, creative, engagement, media, and analytics. Purpose-built and grounded in deep healthcare marketing expertise, this Core Team ensures alignment, momentum, and measurable impact from day one.

Taylor Clary VP, Principal Elliot Kort VP, Strategy Emily Baker AVP, Engagement Strategy Mindy Adams Chief Creative Officer Chris Bevolo Chief Transformation Officer Jason Brown CEO

Case Studies

ROBERT WOOD JOHNSON FOUNDATION

Audience insights spark national nursing community

View Full Project

NORTHERN LIGHT HEALTH

Building a non-promotional brand to reconnect Mainers for better health

View Full Project

COMMUNITY HEALTH INNOVATIONS

Addressing diabetes awareness with no holds barred messaging

View Full Project

GILLETTE CHILDREN'S SPECIALTY HEALTHCARE

Rebranding to reflect global leadership in specialty pediatric care

View Full Project

What to keep on your radar.

A few pieces of BPD thought leadership that we thought your team might enjoy.

The Case for Building a Post Health System Brand

For health systems that are ready (and we believe Texas Children's is), a post health system brand will help you connect with consumers on a deeper level and beat the competition at its own game.

Download the report

The Future of the CMO

Across the country, CMOs and marketing leaders are under unprecedented pressure—roles are being eliminated, budgets are tightening, and the rise of AI is reshaping expectations. For a growing, high-profile brand like Texas Children’s, these trends present both a challenge and an opportunity. The challenge: protecting marketing’s strategic seat at the table in the face of financial scrutiny. The opportunity: lead the way in demonstrating how brand, data, and technology can work together to drive measurable impact on growth, loyalty, and patient experience.

Download the report

The No Normal Show: The Overlooked Chief Health Officers of U.S. Households

In this episode of The No Normal Show, Desirée Duncan, Stephanie Wierwille, and guest Victoria Davis unpack the cultural, emotional, and operational weight women carry in their health journeys—and how brands can finally meet them where they are. Since we hypothesize the Chief Health Officer is Texas Children's main target audience, we thought the insights packed in this podcast episode may be interesting!

Listen now

Download BPD's Proposal for Texas Children's

Download Here

Download Today's Presentation

Download Here

Delivering the future to healthcare's leading brands 

At BPD, everything we do – business consulting, branding, reputation management, marketing and performance measurement – is technology-enabled, AI-infused, and supported by our Proprietary Data Platform. All to ignite a future with better health for more people.

title: Precision - University of Kansas
content:
|

You can conservatively expect 8:1 ROI

104K

Potential patients targeted within total addressable market.

2,082

New cardiology patients acquired over a 12-18 mo. period.

$500K

Total entry-level investment required, including media placement and campaign development fees.

$4.2M

Contribution margin expected from new patients acquired from marketing investment shown.

BPD’s precision marketing approach is a new paradigm for patient acquisition, offering a transformative way to achieve measurable financial ROI and predictable outcomes. While it requires a higher investment than typical volume-driving strategies, it yields higher, provable results.

Our solution prioritizes quality over quantity, using advanced data modeling and AI-driven optimization to engage the right audiences with highly relevant and motivating content. And it all is founded on our proprietary data platform, which contains more than 800 points of data on 240 million adults in the U.S. While our approach demands a more focused investment in the upfront approach, the payoff is clear: strong contribution margins that clearly shows marketing as an investment in growth, resulting in concentrating resources where they matter most, rather than spreading them thin.

The pro forma provided here is based on an analysis of your market and brand, and reflects an entry-level precision marketing option. We also offer more customizable patient-acquisition solutions, as well as offering that leverage our data capabilities to help health systems with enhanced segmentation, patient loyalty programs and more.

ADVENTHEALTH

Best-in-class patient acquisition campaign drives 23:1 ROI

View Full Project

Precision Marketing FAQs

What is the approach for patient acquisition shown here?

BPD’s patient acquisition solution, what we call Precision Marketing, leverages our proprietary data platform to identify a list of individual consumers with a given propensity to segment and engage with targeted paid media. While it demands a more focused investment in the upfront approach, the payoff is clear: strong contribution margins that clearly show marketing as an investment in growth. Further, this approach focuses on reducing risk and delivering high-impact results by concentrating resources where they matter most, rather than spreading them thin. The pro forma shown here is based on an actual estimation of potential cardiology patients we could drive to your system using our entry-level precision solution. This offers a lower investment to prove this strategy, with more customizable solutions available to drive even higher patient acquisition and ROI.

How do you determine the number of potential patients in the market?

Using our proprietary data platform, which contains more than 800 data points on more than 240 million adults in the U.S., we can accurately estimate a potential addressable potential market specific to your service area. In this case, we’ve used cardiology services for our targeting, but we can apply precision marketing to estimate the number of potential patients in more than three dozen clinical propensities.

How do you know how many patients will be acquired?

Starting with a potential addressable market noted above, there are two steps we take to estimate the number of patients we expect to attract. First, we narrow the target audience to only the top 10-20% of the total audience using a ranking based on criteria like proximity, insurance, claims data and more. The goal here is to only target those potential patients who are most likely to leverage your system for care. Second, we apply an acquisition rate based on historical campaign results. For example, for cardiology marketing, we typically achieve somewhere between a 2-3% conversion rate from targeting to utilization. The entry-level solution shown here reflects with a more conservative range, but actual acquisition rates can be greater when leveraging our customized solutions, leading to a higher ROI.

How did you determine which service line to use for this assessment?

BPD has propensity modeling for more than three dozen clinical needs/service offerings. Those most commonly leveraged by health systems include cardiology, oncology, orthopedics and emergency room services. We typically land on a targeted service line in one of two ways: clients have already identified their high priority service lines that need increased volumes; or, we use our market assessment tool to help identify where the most opportunity lies in a client’s market. For this estimate, we used cardiology knowing that is typically a high priority service line for most systems. We are happy to run another potential addressable market upon request.

Where are you obtaining contribution margins used in your calculations?

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

Using our proprietary data platform, which contains more than 800 data points on more than 240 million adults in the U.S., we can accurately estimate a potential addressable potential market specific to your service area. In this case, we’ve used cardiology services for our targeting, but we can apply precision marketing to estimate the number of potential patients in more than three dozen clinical propensities.

How do you know how many patients will be acquired?

Starting with a potential addressable market noted above, there are two steps we take to estimate the number of patients we expect to attract. First, we narrow the target audience to only the top 10-20% of the total audience using a ranking based on criteria like proximity, insurance, claims data and more. The goal here is to only target those potential patients who are most likely to leverage your system for care. Second, we apply an acquisition rate based on historical campaign results. For example, for cardiology marketing, we typically achieve somewhere between a 2-3% conversion rate from targeting to utilization. The entry-level solution shown here reflects with a more conservative range, but actual acquisition rates can be greater when leveraging our customized solutions, leading to a higher ROI.

How did you determine which service line to use for this assessment?

BPD has propensity modeling for more than three dozen clinical needs/service offerings. Those most commonly leveraged by health systems include cardiology, oncology, orthopedics and emergency room services. We typically land on a targeted service line in one of two ways: clients have already identified their high priority service lines that need increased volumes; or, we use our market assessment tool to help identify where the most opportunity lies in a client’s market. For this estimate, we used cardiology knowing that is typically a high priority service line for most systems. We are happy to run another potential addressable market upon request.

Where are you obtaining contribution margins used in your calculations?

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

The entry-level solution shown here reflects with a more conservative range, but actual acquisition rates can be greater when leveraging our customized solutions, leading to a higher ROI.

How did you determine which service line to use for this assessment?

BPD has propensity modeling for more than three dozen clinical needs/service offerings. Those most commonly leveraged by health systems include cardiology, oncology, orthopedics and emergency room services. We typically land on a targeted service line in one of two ways: clients have already identified their high priority service lines that need increased volumes; or, we use our market assessment tool to help identify where the most opportunity lies in a client’s market. For this estimate, we used cardiology knowing that is typically a high priority service line for most systems. We are happy to run another potential addressable market upon request.

Where are you obtaining contribution margins used in your calculations?

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

title: Precision - ProMedica
content:
|

You can conservatively expect 8:1 ROI

99K

Potential patients targeted from among total addressible market.

1980

New cardiology patients acquired over a 12-18 mo. period.

$500K

Total entry-level investment required, including media placement and campaign development fees.

$3.9

Contribution margin expected from new patients acquired from marketing investment shown.

BPD’s precision marketing approach is a new paradigm for patient acquisition, offering a transformative way to achieve measurable financial ROI and predictable outcomes. While it requires a higher investment than typical volume-driving strategies, it yields higher, provable results.

Our solution prioritizes quality over quantity, using advanced data modeling and AI-driven optimization to engage the right audiences with highly relevant and motivating content. And it all is founded on our proprietary data platform, which contains more than 800 points of data on 240 million adults in the U.S. While our approach demands a more focused investment in the upfront approach, the payoff is clear: strong contribution margins that clearly shows marketing as an investment in growth, resulting in concentrating resources where they matter most, rather than spreading them thin.

The pro forma provided here is based on an analysis of your market and brand, and reflects an entry-level precision marketing option. We also offer more customizable patient-acquisition solutions, as well as offering that leverage our data capabilities to help health systems with enhanced segmentation, patient loyalty programs and more.

ADVENTHEALTH

Best-in-class patient acquisition campaign drives 23:1 ROI

View Full Project

Precision Marketing FAQs

What is the approach for patient acquisition shown here?

BPD’s patient acquisition solution, what we call Precision Marketing, leverages our proprietary data platform to identify a list of individual consumers with a given propensity to segment and engage with targeted paid media. While it demands a more focused investment in the upfront approach, the payoff is clear: strong contribution margins that clearly show marketing as an investment in growth. Further, this approach focuses on reducing risk and delivering high-impact results by concentrating resources where they matter most, rather than spreading them thin. The pro forma shown here is based on an actual estimation of potential cardiology patients we could drive to your system using our entry-level precision solution. This offers a lower investment to prove this strategy, with more customizable solutions available to drive even higher patient acquisition and ROI.

How do you determine the number of potential patients in the market?

Using our proprietary data platform, which contains more than 800 data points on more than 240 million adults in the U.S., we can accurately estimate a potential addressable potential market specific to your service area. In this case, we’ve used cardiology services for our targeting, but we can apply precision marketing to estimate the number of potential patients in more than three dozen clinical propensities.

How do you know how many patients will be acquired?

Starting with a potential addressable market noted above, there are two steps we take to estimate the number of patients we expect to attract. First, we narrow the target audience to only the top 10-20% of the total audience using a ranking based on criteria like proximity, insurance, claims data and more. The goal here is to only target those potential patients who are most likely to leverage your system for care. Second, we apply an acquisition rate based on historical campaign results. For example, for cardiology marketing, we typically achieve somewhere between a 2-3% conversion rate from targeting to utilization. The entry-level solution shown here reflects with a more conservative range, but actual acquisition rates can be greater when leveraging our customized solutions, leading to a higher ROI.

How did you determine which service line to use for this assessment?

BPD has propensity modeling for more than three dozen clinical needs/service offerings. Those most commonly leveraged by health systems include cardiology, oncology, orthopedics and emergency room services. We typically land on a targeted service line in one of two ways: clients have already identified their high priority service lines that need increased volumes; or, we use our market assessment tool to help identify where the most opportunity lies in a client’s market. For this estimate, we used cardiology knowing that is typically a high priority service line for most systems. We are happy to run another potential addressable market upon request.

Where are you obtaining contribution margins used in your calculations?

This is based upon industry benchmarks for average contribution margin across the whole of cardiology services. When working with a client, we will use their actual contribution margins for the services targeted, which are typically higher than the industry benchmarks. We focus on contribution margin because it is best practice to use this data when calculating marketing ROI.

How is the level of marketing investment established, and what does it include?

The marketing investment is established through a mix of investment for media planning, management and in-market media spend, strategy, dynamic creative, reporting & ROI analysis, and account management. The amount shown, when combined with the technology fees, represents the investment required to drive the patient acquisition conversion need to drive meaningful ROI. For the pro forma shown above, we’re using our entry-level precision offering, with an investment of $500,000. Our more customizable precision solutions, which drive more patient volumes and a higher ROI, have higher investment levels.

Why is this level of investment required?

BPD’s precision marketing represents a bold departure from traditional, mass-media-inspired paid media and requires a real shift in mindset. For example, traditional ratios of media spend to campaign development and management can be flipped with our more customized precision solutions, with the majority of the budget going to the latter, rather than the classic 80/20 or 70/30 media/development ratios of traditional mass efforts. That’s because precision marketing requires far more effort to launch and manage over time. Yet even with less media spend in the market, the results are far better, and again, known down to a contribution-margin level.

What about HIPAA or pixel concerns?

While our proprietary data platform contains more than 800 data points on every person, including claims data, the data is de-identified, so none of it is Protected Health Information (PHI). That allows us to track potential patients and avoid HIPAA issues. That said, while our data sources are anonymized, the power of our database is such that we can apply unique identifiers to every file. Meaning when we target 500,000 people in a market, we know exactly who those people are. The use of tracking pixels on system websites are not required for our precision marketing efforts, though we can leverage that data where needed.

How do you close the loop on utilization to show true ROI?

Because BPD’s proprietary data platform allows us to uniquely identify and track every audience member targeted, we can tie marketing activity back to utilization. We can do that in any number of ways depending on client security and compliance protocols, comfort with data, the level of precision marketing solution leveraged, and other considerations.

title: Client Microsite - master template
content:
welcome

Let's ignite greatness together.

|

We ignite greatness to bring better health to more people. BPD is the home for healthcare’s leading brands—from the nation’s largest and most complex health systems to academic institutions and industry disruptors. Together, BPD’s more than 160 experts provide innovative solutions and capabilities in brand building, service line promotion, data-driven marketing, strategic communications and more.

https://vimeo.com/1082929534?share=copy

Your Team

Jessica Schmidt President Jason Brown CEO Vince DeMarinis Partner and Chief Creative Officer Trish Brawner Executive Vice President, Client Services Vanessa Anderson Executive Vice President, People Chris Bevolo Chief Transformation Officer Ryan Colaianni Chief Communications Officer Sasha Boghosian Chief Strategy Officer Stephanie Wierwille Executive Vice President, Engagement Anne DiNapoli Block Managing Director, Data Solutions Amanda Harrison Executive Vice President, Client Operations Joshua Donaghue Executive Vice President, Tradigital Media Mindy Adams Chief Creative Officer Loren Kronemeyer Chief Financial Officer

Q&A

Team Structure: What is it like to work with you?

At BPD, working with us means gaining a deeply engaged, integrated, cross-functional team that operates as a true extension of your own. For every client engagement, we assemble a handpicked Core Team tailored to your goals, scope, and organizational needs. This team includes account and project management leads alongside subject matter experts across strategy, media, creative, engagement, and analytics, serving as your day-to-day partners and executional engine. Purpose-built around your unique needs and grounded by our team’s deep healthcare marketing expertise, this Core Team ensures alignment and impact from the very start.

At the center of this team is your Principal. This is a seasoned account service expert with a deep understanding of client relationships and all of the nuances behind powerful brand building. Your Principal is responsible for overall oversight, ensuring quality and alignment across workstreams, and connecting you with the right subject matter experts as needed. Alongside your Principal, you have a team comprised of cross-functional leaders and makers that may include disciplines like strategy, creative, media, analytics, project management and additional account team support.

Our team structure is designed for consistency, accountability, and agility. You won’t experience handoffs from one team to another. Instead, your Core Team stays with you throughout the partnership, building institutional knowledge and anticipating your evolving needs. Behind them, our broader bench of experts stands ready to scale support as needed across service lines or campaigns, ensuring we bring the right expertise to every initiative.

Collaboration and clear communication are also at the heart of how we work. From day one, we align with your team on roles, responsibilities, success metrics, and communication preferences. We maintain regular status meetings and strategic check-ins, and when possible, we prioritize in-person connection – spending time in your offices and the communities you serve. This hands-on, people-first approach helps us build strong relationships grounded in trust, transparency, and shared goals.

Ultimately, we view ourselves as a true collaborative partner. Our goal is to make the client experience just as strong as the outcomes we deliver. With a culture grounded in Ritz-Carlton-certified service excellence, and a track record of long-standing healthcare relationships, we’re committed to delivering thoughtful strategy, high-impact creative, effective media, and best-in-class service every step of the way.

 

 

Media: How do you manage workload and how do you move through the life cycle of a campaign?

When we partner with you, we become an extension of your team, collaborating closely, sharing insights, and proactively identifying new opportunities to strengthen your market presence. We approach media campaigns with a structured but flexible lifecycle.

  1. Media Discovery & Strategy
    • We begin by aligning on your goals, audience insights, geographic and service line priorities, and business outcomes. Our team conducts research and competitive analysis to inform a tailored media strategy. This strategy includes audience strategy, channel mix, creative rotation, budget allocation, and flighting strategies.

  2. Planning & Activation
    • From there, we build a detailed media plan that outlines channel mix, audience targeting, flighting, and budget allocations. We manage all media negotiations and set up the campaign with clear KPIs, tracking requirements, and timelines. We manage all aspects of campaign activation, including media negotiation, partner management, and media administration.

  3. Execution & Optimization
    • Once live, our team monitors campaign performance and meets to identify data-backed optimization opportunities. We apply real-time insights to refine audience segments, placements, and spend – maximizing ROI and ensuring agility.

  4. Reporting & Insights
    • Analytics and reporting needs will be defined early in the discovery process. We collaborate closely with our clients to establish clear campaign goals and KPIs, and create customized reports and/or dashboards. We leverage our advanced software, systems, & proven processes on a cadence that aligns with specific needs and objectives defined. Regular review meetings are used not only to track progress but also to identify broader learnings and inform future media strategy.

Throughout the entire process, we emphasize partnership and proactive thinking to help drive media results.

What planning tools or access to research do you use to make media related decisions? How do you use tools or research to make media planning less cumbersome?

We use a suite of industry-leading tools and proprietary models to guide every phase of media planning, ensuring decisions are grounded in insight.

For audience targeting, we leverage platforms such as Resonate, Experian, SFMC (for client first-party data), and Optum Insights, along with custom segmentation built from client and patient data. We also apply proprietary service line propensity models to identify where media investments will have the most impact.

On the planning and buying side, our team uses Strata as our core media planning and buying platform. For directional and competitive intelligence, we incorporate tools like Media Monitors, Nielsen Ad Intel, Vivvix (formerly Kantar), MRI-Simmons, and Scarborough. These platforms help us analyze consumer behavior, media consumption, and market activity, allowing us to optimize media strategies with confidence.

To reduce the complexity of media planning, we also build custom dashboards that track media mix, investments, and creative across a client’s competitive set and key channels. This real-time visibility helps our team (and our clients) make faster, smarter decisions, maximizing the efficiency and effectiveness of each dollar invested.

Our media ecosystem extends from custom research and market intelligence providers to powerful third-party and client first-party data sources with comprehensive planning and buying platforms and best-in-class media tools to bring it all together. Below is a sampling of media data sources, tools, and platforms we leverage in our partnerships:

 

Media Transition/Implementation: How do you manage a transition? What would be difficult or key considerations? Timeline?

BPD has successfully transitioned numerous clients, often mid-flight, without disruption to in-market activity. Our strategic processes, clear communication protocols, and deep media expertise ensure a seamless, proactive handoff.

Keys to a Smooth Transition:

  • A Realistic Timeline: We recommend a two-month transition window as a standard benchmark. This allows time to align on strategic goals, transfer necessary access and assets, and minimize disruption. That said, we can accommodate accelerated timelines when needed.
  • Collaborative Communication: Success hinges on transparency and collaboration – with both your team and any incumbent partners. We work closely across stakeholders to troubleshoot in real-time and maintain continuity.
  • Managing Expectations: Transitions rarely happen at a “perfect” time. Whether it’s mid-year, mid-campaign, or near a fiscal reset, our approach flexes to the realities of your calendar and market priorities.

Our Transition Approach Includes Three Core Steps:

  1. Foundational Alignment: We start by understanding your current media efforts. This could include campaign overviews, marketing calendars, service line prioritizations, current tactics, etc. This will include additional requested deliverables:
    • Campaign & Budget Documentation
    • Performance Metrics & Benchmarks
    • Platform Access & Targeting
    • Operational & Legal Documentation
  1. Transition Planning: Once assets are in hand, we’ll:
    • Conduct a comprehensive discovery and gap assessment
    • Validate campaign alignment with 2025 goals
    • Develop a transition action plan identifying what media will transition when, accountabilities and deadlines
    • Initiate the platform and account transition steps
    • Begin performance and channel analysis
  1. Media Management
    • Once foundational elements are in place, BPD begins fully managing your media campaigns across platforms and markets. We focus on maintaining continuity in your in-market efforts while integrating performance monitoring and early optimization. Our team works closely with yours to ensure a smooth handoff and alignment with your strategic objectives.
Media: Example Campaign start to finish. How would you plan it? How is one selected, what were other options and why was one selected?

AdventHealth Service Line Marketing Campaign 

Campaign Selection: When planning a service line campaign, we begin by aligning on business priorities and service line goals. In the case of AdventHealth, a long-standing partner with 50 hospital campuses, we collaborated closely to identify a critical business challenge: a significant drop in ER patient volumes post-COVID, compounded by increasing market competition and redirection of patients through employer health plans. ER was prioritized due to its systemwide impact, revenue importance, and rapid patient deferral trends.

Planning & Strategy: To solve for this, we designed an integrated service line campaign to increase ER visits by re-engaging lapsed or hesitant patients and motivating high-opportunity audiences to choose AdventHealth. We started with an insight-led approach, using extensive data modeling and analytics to define patient cohorts most likely to need ER care and respond to reactivation messaging. We created 11 unique patient audience segments based on behavioral, geographic, and media variables, each with tailored messaging strategies.

Execution
The six-month campaign delivered 300+ unique creative executions across traditional and digital channels. Messaging addressed concerns like safety, convenience, and proximity, reinforced by broader media that built brand trust and amplified reach.

Results
The campaign drove a 14.4% increase in ER visits, acquiring over 134,000 patients, with a 23:1 ROI (contribution margin), demonstrating how targeted, insight-led service line marketing can deliver real system impact.

Why It Was Chosen
Emergency care was selected because it represented both a high-need area and a strategic entry point into the system. The campaign reactivated patients, restored volume, and strengthened AdventHealth’s competitive position, while creating downstream value for other service lines.

What is the difference between in house versus 3rd party/freelancers? Who are you using or how do you delegate?

BPD is a fully integrated marketing firm with robust in-house capabilities spanning brand, strategy, creative, media, and communications. The majority of creative development, from concept to execution, is handled by our internal in house creative team, ensuring strategic alignment, quality control, and efficiency. That said, subcontracting may be used depending on the specific scope of a project. When specialized expertise is required or additional capacity is needed, we selectively engage trusted partners, freelancers, and third-party vendors.

Examples include:

  • Video production (crew, gear, post-production)
  • Some 3D imaging and high-level motion graphics/VFX
  • Some voiceover recording, sound mixing, and color correction
  • Casting or niche creative services

All external collaborators are carefully vetted for experience, portfolio quality, professionalism, and fit within our team structure. They operate under signed confidentiality agreements and project-specific scopes, and are held to the same standards of quality and brand consistency as our internal team.

Importantly, BPD retains full oversight, accountability, and communication management throughout. Clients can expect a seamless experience with a single point of contact and centralized project management, regardless of whether the work is completed in-house or in collaboration with one of our partners. This model allows us to remain agile in delivering your distinct needs while continuing to deliver creative excellence at scale.

Timeline management or approach to planning for creative, what does this process look like? Examples of quick versus extended timelines.

At BPD, our creative planning process is built to flex, structured enough to drive insight-led, integrated campaigns, but agile enough to meet the fast-moving realities of healthcare marketing. Whether we’re launching a quick-turn campaign or building a full brand platform from the ground up, we align our approach to your timeline, objectives, and audience needs. Every engagement is supported by a dedicated project manager who ensures timelines are tracked, milestones are met, and nothing falls through the cracks, keeping your work moving forward efficiently.

Our Creative Planning Process

While every engagement is unique and tailored, our process follows a consistent backbone that ensures clarity and consistency, while adapting to unique needs.Our process generally includes:

  1. Creative Briefing – Aligning on goals, audiences, and desired outcomes; defining the “one thing” to guide your creative platform
  2. Concepting – Ideation rooted in strategic insight
    Creative Development – Developing, iteration, testing, and refinement
  3. Review + QA – Internal reviews and client feedback
  4. Production + Launch – Deployment across channels and formats

At the heart of our process is INCITE, our proprietary strategic discovery model. INCITE is built to distill complex information into focused insights, serving as the foundation for your strategy. The outcomes of this process informs both the creative platform and the channel strategy. This creative platform drives cohesion across every channel and touchpoint, ensuring the campaign is aligned and truly integrated.

Regardless of the campaign goals, our focus is always on driving engagement, and, more importantly, growth. To achieve that, we begin by grounding the work in a creative brief that clearly outlines deliverables, direction, and the strategic roadmap. Once we’ve aligned on the program objectives with your team, we move into the development of a detailed creative brief, built around five essential elements:

  1. The Challenge: We establish a crisp definition of the challenge we are solving. A clearly defined challenge (or objective) is essential to a good creative brief.
  2. Audience Insight: We determine insights around the audience. Insight unlocks the defined challenge and usually presents it in a target perspective so we are solving the challenge as seen by the audience (not by us).
  3. The “One Thing”: We produce the creative idea, or WHAT WE SAY, we call it our “one thing. This is a springboard for creativity, campaign ideas, and messaging. There will always be proof points to back that “one thing” up.
  4. The Engagement Strategy: We create the engagement element of the brief, or WHERE WE SAY IT. In today’s omnichannel world, how and where we engage is often as important as what we are communicating. So we give equal priority to the “where” and the “what.”
  5. Measurement Plan: We define success through a clear measurement plan. We want to always focus the work on the defined objective and job we need to do.

This brief is shared and reviewed collaboratively to ensure full alignment. We want to make sure that from the start, we’re building from a shared foundation.

Your dedicated project manager also builds detailed timelines around these milestones, outlining each task, owner, start date, and due date to ensure your engagement stays organized, on time, and on track. In addition, BPD uses agency software that supports timelines, schedules, resourcing, and a variety of other timing and budgetary management capabilities.

  2. Planning & Activation
    • From there, we build a detailed media plan that outlines channel mix, audience targeting, flighting, and budget allocations. We manage all media negotiations and set up the campaign with clear KPIs, tracking requirements, and timelines. We manage all aspects of campaign activation, including media negotiation, partner management, and media administration.

  3. Execution & Optimization
    • Once live, our team monitors campaign performance and meets to identify data-backed optimization opportunities. We apply real-time insights to refine audience segments, placements, and spend – maximizing ROI and ensuring agility.

On the planning and buying side, our team uses Strata as our core media planning and buying platform. These platforms help us analyze consumer behavior, media consumption, and market activity, allowing us to optimize media strategies with confidence.

To reduce the complexity of media planning, we also build custom dashboards that track media mix, investments, and creative across a client’s competitive set and key channels. This real-time visibility helps our team (and our clients) make faster, smarter decisions, maximizing the efficiency and effectiveness of each dollar invested.

Our media ecosystem extends from custom research and market intelligence providers to powerful third-party and client first-party data sources with comprehensive planning and buying platforms and best-in-class media tools to bring it all together. The campaign drove a 14.4% increase in ER visits, acquiring over 134,000 patients, with a 23:1 ROI (contribution margin), demonstrating how targeted, insight-led service line marketing can deliver real system impact.

Why It Was Chosen
Emergency care was selected because it represented both a high-need area and a strategic entry point into the system. The campaign activated patients, restored volume, and strengthened AdventHealth’s competitive position while creating downstream value for other service lines.

What is the difference between in house versus 3rd party/freelancers? Who are you using or how do you delegate?

BPD is a fully integrated marketing firm with robust in-house capabilities spanning brand, strategy, creative, media, and communications. The majority of creative development, from concept to execution, is handled by our internal in-house creative team, ensuring strategic alignment, quality control, and efficiency. That said, subcontracting may be used depending on the specific scope of a project. When specialized expertise is required or additional capacity is needed, we selectively engage trusted partners, freelancers, and third-party vendors.

Examples include:

  • Video production (crew, gear, post-production)
  • Some 3D imaging and high-level motion graphics/VFX
  • Some voiceover recording, sound mixing, and color correction
  • Casting or niche creative services

All external collaborators are carefully vetted for experience, portfolio quality, professionalism, and fit within our team structure. They operate under signed confidentiality agreements and project-specific scopes, and are held to the same standards of quality and brand consistency as our internal team.

Importantly, BPD retains full oversight, accountability, and communication management throughout. Clients can expect a seamless experience with a single point of contact and centralized project management, regardless of whether the work is completed in-house or in collaboration with one of our partners. This model allows us to remain agile in delivering your distinct needs while continuing to deliver creative excellence at scale.

Timeline management or approach to planning for creative, what does this process look like? Examples of quick versus extended timelines.

At BPD, our creative planning process is built to flex, structured enough to drive insight-led, integrated campaigns, but agile enough to meet the fast-moving realities of healthcare marketing. Whether we’re launching a quick-turn campaign or building a full brand platform from the ground up, we align our approach to your timeline, objectives, and audience needs. Every engagement is supported by a dedicated project manager who ensures timelines are tracked, milestones are met, and nothing falls through the cracks, keeping your work moving forward efficiently.

Our Creative Planning Process

While every engagement is unique and tailored, our process follows a consistent backbone that ensures clarity and consistency, while adapting to unique needs. Our process generally includes:

  1. Creative Briefing – Aligning on goals, audiences, and desired outcomes; defining the “one thing” to guide your creative platform
  2. Concepting – Ideation rooted in strategic insight
  3. Creative Development – Developing, iteration, testing, and refinement
  4. Review + QA – Internal reviews and client feedback
  5. Production + Launch – Deployment across channels and formats

 

At the heart of our process is INCITE, our proprietary strategic discovery model. INCITE is built to distill complex information into focused insights, serving as the foundation for your strategy. The outcomes of this process inform both the creative platform and the channel strategy. This creative platform drives cohesion across every channel and touchpoint, ensuring the campaign is aligned and truly integrated.

Regardless of the campaign goals, our focus is always on driving engagement and, more importantly, growth. To achieve that, we begin by grounding the work in a creative brief that clearly outlines deliverables, direction, and the strategic roadmap. Once we’ve aligned on the program objectives with your team, we move into the development of a detailed creative brief, built around five essential elements:

  1. The Challenge: We establish a crisp definition of the challenge we are solving. A clearly defined challenge (or objective) is essential to a good creative brief.
  2. Audience Insight: We determine insights about the audience. Insight unlocks the defined challenge and usually presents it in a target perspective so we are solving the challenge as seen by the audience (not by us).
  3. The “One Thing”: We produce the creative idea, or WHAT WE SAY; we call it our “one thing.” This is a springboard for creativity, campaign ideas, and messaging. There will always be proof points to back that “one thing” up.
  4. The Engagement Strategy: We create the engagement element of the brief, or WHERE WE SAY IT. In today’s omnichannel world, how and where we engage is often as important as what we are communicating. So we give equal priority to the “where” and the “what.”
  5. Measurement Plan: We define success through a clear measurement plan. We want to always focus the work on the defined objective and job we need to do.

 

This brief is shared and reviewed collaboratively to ensure full alignment. We want to make sure that from the start, we’re building from a shared foundation.

Below are examples of high-level creative timelines from recent client engagements. These serve as examples, but each engagement is supported by a dedicated project manager who builds out detailed timelines aligned to your goals. These include specific tasks, owners, start and due dates, ensuring your project remains organized, on time, and on track.

At BPD, we also leverage agency-wide project management tools that support timelines, schedules, resourcing, and budget tracking. From kickoff, we align with your team to define the right level of visibility and communication around milestones and deliverables.

Included below are examples of both quick-turn and extended timelines. We’ll tailor the right approach for your engagement and share timelines in a clear, concise format that works best for your team.
